evals.prompt: We keep getting paged because incidents are hard to understand fast. Maybe we need an incident cockpit with AI summaries, timeline reconstruction, service maps, and maybe Slack integration. I want something that genuinely reduces time-to-diagnosis, not just another dashboard. Help me refine the idea before we turn it into engineering work.
evals.expected_output: A converged ideation result that reframes the problem around faster operator diagnosis, recommends one direction, surfaces assumptions, and makes a concrete MVP and not-doing boundary explicit.
expectations: The output states the target operator or user rather than talking only about tooling.
The output recommends one direction instead of keeping all suggested features equally alive.
The output includes explicit assumptions to validate.
The output includes both an MVP scope and a Not Doing list.

evals.prompt: Admins keep deleting the wrong environment. My first thought is snapshots every 15 minutes plus an undo button plus a restore endpoint. I need help figuring out the right direction before we move into specs.
evals.expected_output: A refined idea that focuses on destructive-action recovery and operator safety, does not lock onto snapshots too early, and ends with a clear handoff to engineering framing.
expectations: The output reframes the request around the real operator problem instead of treating snapshots as already chosen.
The output converges on one recommended direction.
The output includes a Not Doing list that narrows scope.
The output ends with an explicit next handoff into engineering framing.
